title: The Cavalry of Rome
designIntent: DSK-composed WebGPU/WebGL-first Roman strategy route: scan over a large procedural campaign map, issue one world action at a time, read supply pressure, march corridors, morale fronts, unit cohesion, and command choices through renderer-neutral descriptors, then preserve the older battlefield and tactical maneuver surfaces without moving reusable logic into the renderer.
standingIterationRule: Every future gameplay iteration should improve visual fidelity as a secondary goal and should not regress terrain density, vegetation descriptors, lighting mood, region readability, soldier fidelity, hex readability, maneuver feedback, command readability, or cinematic composition.

currentGameplayStep.intent: Add a deeper strategic-readability layer around the campaign map so the player can understand supply lines, possible march corridors, cohesion, morale pressure, and command state without putting tactical truth into the renderer.
currentGameplayStep.implementation: cavalry-campaign-fractal-domain-kit.js emits supply-line, march-corridor, unit-cohesion, morale-weather, maneuver-choice, and renderer-handoff descriptors from the current campaign state. cavalry-campaign-fractal-handoff-pass.js imports NexusEngine main via CDN, computes the descriptor domain each frame, exposes GameHost campaignFractal state, and renders a presentation-only overlay that consumes descriptors without owning campaign simulation, input, WebGL, audio, assets, or frame-loop truth.

advanceLeft.effect: Roll 1d6 and move that many eligible Rome units from the left third.

advanceCenter.effect: Roll 1d6 and move that many eligible Rome units from the center third.

advanceRight.effect: Roll 1d6 and move that many eligible Rome units from the right third.

lineBrigade.effect: Move a connected adjacent group of Rome units.

berserk.effect: Move one Rome unit up to two spaces and attack an adjacent enemy.

scout.effect: Move one Rome unit up to three spaces.

campaignFractalDomain.tree: cavalry-campaign-fractal-domain -> campaign-readability/supply-line + march-corridor -> battlefield-readability/unit-cohesion + morale-weather + maneuver-choice -> renderer-handoff/descriptors-only
campaignFractalDomain.handoff: renderer consumes descriptor buckets only: supplyLines, marchCorridors, cohesionFields, moraleFronts, maneuverChoices

fidelityFocus.terrain: realistic low-poly terrain with domain warping, FBM/ridged noise, blended biome colors, moisture/slope/river masks, and non-repeating landforms
fidelityFocus.mapNavigation: large pannable campaign surface with keyboard/drag pan and wheel zoom
fidelityFocus.campaignReadability: descriptor-driven supply lines, march corridors, unit cohesion fields, morale-weather fronts, and command choice bands over the campaign map
fidelityFocus.regions: oversized highlighted region meshes with glow, wobble borders, and DSK affordance descriptors
fidelityFocus.visibleUi: minimal route chrome; reusable command/readability logic remains descriptor-owned
fidelityFocus.uiDom: presentation-only sinks and command surfaces; campaign truth stays in the campaign state and domain descriptors

proceduralVegetation.grass: deterministic grass descriptor field is retained as metadata for future terrain-anchored instancing
proceduralVegetation.screenSpaceRendering: disabled because it was visually floating in the sky and caused panning/selection jank
proceduralVegetation.battlefieldGrounding: tile features draw directly on the hex playfield so they cannot detach from terrain
proceduralVegetation.boundary: local procedural vegetation descriptor DSK candidate owns placements; no screen-space overlay is rendered until true terrain-anchored WebGPU instancing exists

fidelityFocus.soldiers: tactical hex mode uses mini low-poly soldier squads with individual angled shadows, body class colors, military bands, shields, spears, helmets, and selection pennants
fidelityFocus.battlefield: aligned WebGL2-shaded hex grid, terrain tiles, scene-native highlights, shaded dice, aggregated squad pieces, region terrain variation, and no circular token rings

protectedExpressionGuardrails: Do not copy tabletop scenario layouts, card systems, dice faces, command sections, rule text, or unit-stat tables.
Keep the route visual-first until the cinematic terrain and battlefield proof is stable.
Use Roman-history flavor as original visual composition, not as a digital recreation of a board-game ruleset.
currentLocalSurfaces: realistic terrain mesh color and brush-stroke overlay generation
large pannable region ellipse placement and labels
procedural vegetation descriptor generation without screen-space presentation
Rome-perspective aligned WebGL2 hex battlefield overlay
campaign map cell ownership and one-action cadence

rendererOwnership.hexGameplayOverlay: scene-native movement/highlight/squad renderer and first playable interaction layer
rendererOwnership.campaignFractalOverlay: presentation-only Canvas2D consumer of renderer-neutral descriptors
dskOwned: procedural vegetation descriptor snapshot via local DSK candidate
hex battlefield snapshot via local DSK candidate
tactical gameplay snapshot via local DSK candidate
campaign fractal descriptor snapshot via local DSK candidate

futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.reason: Reusable frontier pressure and supply readability for turn-based strategy maps.
Reusable adjacent-move and attack outcome preview descriptors.
Reusable formation density and troop power readability.
Reusable ownership pressure and front-line weather descriptors.
Reusable command availability descriptors independent of DOM buttons.

intentionallyOmitted: unit stat tables beyond visual class and terrain metadata
nextLedge: Promote the campaign fractal kits into reusable ProtoKits once browser validation confirms the descriptors improve command readability without creating overlay clutter.
